edges.description: ACOX1 deficiency blocks peroxisomal VLCFA oxidation.
VLCFAs can induce IL-1 cytokine expression and inflammatory signaling in patient fibroblast models.
VLCFA accumulation contributes to inflammatory demyelination and progressive CNS disease.
The ACOX1 deficiency phenotype spectrum includes retinal degeneration with retinitis pigmentosa and vision abnormalities.
Facial dysmorphism is part of the broader reported ACOX1 deficiency phenotype spectrum.
Hepatic dysfunction is part of the broader reported ACOX1 deficiency phenotype spectrum.
Adrenal insufficiency is part of the broader reported ACOX1 deficiency phenotype spectrum.
Peripheral neuropathy is reported in severe ACOX1 deficiency.
IL-1-centered inflammation is a plausible contributor to inflammatory demyelination.
Pathological microglial programs may amplify neuroinflammation and white-matter injury.
Inflammatory demyelination and progressive white-matter injury produce the leukodystrophy phenotype.
Progressive demyelination is visible as cerebral, cerebellar, brainstem, and subcortical white-matter abnormalities on MRI.
edges.target: Cerebral and Cerebellar White Matter Abnormalities

edges.description: Progressive neurodegeneration causes loss of acquired developmental skills in early childhood.
intermediate_mechanisms: demyelination, neuronal loss, and axonal degeneration

edges.description: Infantile CNS dysfunction presents early with hypotonia.
Infantile CNS involvement includes seizures during the early disease phase.
Early neurologic disease delays acquisition of psychomotor milestones before regression.
Progressive neurodegeneration can lead to loss of visual function during regression.
Progressive neurodegeneration can lead to loss of hearing during regression.
Retinal degeneration contributes to visual impairment in the sensory branch of ACOX1 deficiency.
Elevated VLCFA measurements are the diagnostic biochemical readout of the VLCFA accumulation node.
Low C26:0 oxidation in fibroblasts reports the impaired ACOX1 straight-chain acyl-CoA oxidase activity.
Normal phytanic acid, pristanic acid, and plasmalogen screening markers help distinguish isolated ACOX1 beta-oxidation deficiency from broader peroxisomal dysfunction but do not exclude disease.
edges.target: Preserved phytanic acid, pristanic acid, and plasmalogen screening markers

nodes.description: A homozygous acceptor splice-site variant was identified in a fatal infantile case after fibroblast biochemical testing.

evidence.reference_title: Pitfall in metabolic screening in a patient with fatal peroxisomal beta-oxidation defect.
evidence.snippet: Enzymatic studies in these fibroblasts pointed to peroxisomal acyl-CoA oxidase deficiency and subsequent molecular analyses revealed a homozygous acceptor splice site mutation IVS3-1G>A in the ACOX1 gene (MIM *609751).

nodes.description: Biallelic ACOX1 pathogenic variants reduce straight-chain acyl-CoA oxidase activity, disrupting the first acyl-CoA oxidase step of peroxisomal very-long-chain fatty-acid beta-oxidation.

biological_processes: fatty acid beta-oxidation using acyl-CoA oxidase

evidence.reference_title: Clinical, biochemical, and mutational spectrum of peroxisomal acyl-coenzyme A oxidase deficiency.
evidence.snippet: Peroxisomal acyl-coenzyme A (acyl-CoA) oxidase deficiency is an autosomal recessive inborn error of peroxisomal fatty acid oxidation due to a deficiency of straight-chain acyl-CoA oxidase (SCOX).

evidence.reference_title: Peroxisomal acyl-CoA-oxidase deficiency: two new cases.
evidence.snippet: Studies in fibroblasts from the two patients revealed a deficiency of one of the two peroxisomal acyl-CoA oxidases, that is, straight-chain acyl-CoA oxidase (ACOX1).

nodes.description: A homozygous deletion removing a large part of intron 3 and exons 4-14 was reported in one patient.

evidence.reference_title: Peroxisomal acyl-CoA-oxidase deficiency: two new cases.
evidence.snippet: Subsequent molecular analysis of ACOX1 showed a homozygous deletion, which removes a large part of intron 3 and exons 4-14 in the first patient.

meta.association: Biallelic loss-of-function variants cause autosomal recessive ACOX1 deficiency.

evidence.reference_title: Clinical, biochemical, and mutational spectrum of peroxisomal acyl-coenzyme A oxidase deficiency.
evidence.snippet: No clear genotype-phenotype correlation was observed.

nodes.description: Mutant BV-2 microglial cell lines model peroxisomal beta-oxidation defects, VLCFA accumulation, lipid/immune/autophagy reprogramming, and a disease-associated microglial signature.

evidence.reference_title: Peroxisomal defects in microglial cells induce a disease-associated microglial signature.
evidence.snippet: We previously established BV-2 microglial cell models bearing mutations in peroxisomal genes that recapitulate some of the hallmarks of the peroxisomal β-oxidation defects such as very long-chain fatty acid (VLCFA) accumulation.

nodes.description: Adrenal insufficiency is a less frequently reported manifestation.

evidence.reference_title: Effects of hematopoietic stem cell transplantation on acyl-CoA oxidase deficiency: a sibling comparison study.
evidence.snippet: facial dysmorphism, hepatic dysfunction, and adrenal insufficiency are less frequently reported findings.

nodes.description: MRI can show cerebral and cerebellar white-matter abnormalities and progressive demyelinating lesions.
nodes.id: Cerebral and Cerebellar White Matter Abnormalities

evidence.reference_title: Pitfall in metabolic screening in a patient with fatal peroxisomal beta-oxidation defect.
evidence.snippet: The patient presented with a typical MRI pattern showing pachygyria, perisylvian polymicrogyria, cerebral and cerebellar white matter abnormalities, and facial dysmorphia, progressive psychomotor retardation, deafness, retinopathy, peripheral neuropathy, and infantile seizures strongly indicative for a peroxisomal disorder.

evidence.reference_title: Effects of hematopoietic stem cell transplantation on acyl-CoA oxidase deficiency: a sibling comparison study.
evidence.snippet: Serial brain magnetic resonance imaging in the younger sibling indicated demyelination began in the medulla and progressed rostrally to include the white matter of the cerebellum, pons, midbrain, and eventually subcortical white matter.

evidence.reference_title: Pitfall in metabolic screening in a patient with fatal peroxisomal beta-oxidation defect.
evidence.snippet: Subsequent biochemical investigation in cultured skin fibroblasts of the patient, however, revealed elevated concentrations of VLCFAs, deficient oxidation of C26:0, but normal oxidation of both phytanic acid and pristanic acid and normal DE NOVO plasmalogen synthesis, indicative for a defect in the peroxisomal beta-oxidation system.

readouts.interpretation: Low C26:0 oxidation in fibroblasts reports the impaired ACOX1 straight-chain acyl-CoA oxidase activity.

nodes.description: Progressive neurologic disease leads to loss of acquired motor and communication abilities in early childhood.

evidence.reference_title: Effects of hematopoietic stem cell transplantation on acyl-CoA oxidase deficiency: a sibling comparison study.
evidence.snippet: the natural history of the disease follows a fairly consistent pattern of infantile-onset hypotonia, seizures, delayed acquisition of early developmental milestones, followed by rapid developmental regression between 24 and 48 months of age.

nodes.description: Hypotonia is an early infantile feature of ACOX1 deficiency.

evidence.reference_title: Peroxisomal acyl-CoA-oxidase deficiency: two new cases.
evidence.snippet: Early onset hypotonia, seizures and psychomotor delay were observed in both cases.

evidence.reference_title: Clinical, biochemical, and mutational spectrum of peroxisomal acyl-coenzyme A oxidase deficiency.
evidence.snippet: The biochemical hallmark of this disorder is the accumulation of very long-chain fatty acids.

evidence.reference_title: Pitfall in metabolic screening in a patient with fatal peroxisomal beta-oxidation defect.
evidence.snippet: Subsequent biochemical investigation in cultured skin fibroblasts of the patient, however, revealed elevated concentrations of VLCFAs, deficient oxidation of C26:0, but normal oxidation of both phytanic acid and pristanic acid and normal DE NOVO plasmalogen synthesis, indicative for a defect in the peroxisomal beta-oxidation system.

readouts.interpretation: Elevated VLCFA measurements are the diagnostic biochemical readout of the VLCFA accumulation node.

nodes.description: Facial dysmorphism is a less frequently reported manifestation.

evidence.reference_title: Effects of hematopoietic stem cell transplantation on acyl-CoA oxidase deficiency: a sibling comparison study.
evidence.snippet: facial dysmorphism, hepatic dysfunction, and adrenal insufficiency are less frequently reported findings.

nodes.description: Patient fibroblast transcriptomic and PCR-array studies show IL-1 pathway activation with increased IL-6 and IL-8 secretion, linking ACOX1 loss and VLCFA exposure to inflammatory signaling.

evidence.reference_title: The inflammatory response in acyl-CoA oxidase 1 deficiency (pseudoneonatal adrenoleukodystrophy).
evidence.snippet: Our results show the activation of IL-1 inflammatory pathway accompanied by the increased secretion of two IL-1 target genes, IL-6 and IL-8 cytokines.

evidence.reference_title: The inflammatory response in acyl-CoA oxidase 1 deficiency (pseudoneonatal adrenoleukodystrophy).
evidence.snippet: Thus, the absence of acyl-coenzyme A oxidase 1 activity in P-NALD fibroblasts triggers an inflammatory process, in which the IL-1 pathway seems to be central.

experimental_models.description: Patient fibroblasts assay IL-1, IL-6, and IL-8 inflammatory signaling caused by ACOX1 deficiency.
experimental_models.name: Patient fibroblast inflammatory response model

nodes.description: Hearing loss is reported during the neurodegenerative course.

evidence.reference_title: Effects of hematopoietic stem cell transplantation on acyl-CoA oxidase deficiency: a sibling comparison study.
evidence.snippet: Retinitis pigmentosa, vision abnormalities, and hearing loss are also common manifestations

nodes.description: HSCT has been attempted in ACOX1 deficiency but did not halt the neurodegenerative course; available evidence suggests possible reduction of white-matter inflammation rather than disease arrest.

evidence.reference_title: Effects of hematopoietic stem cell transplantation on acyl-CoA oxidase deficiency: a sibling comparison study.
evidence.snippet: Although HSCT did not halt the course of ACOX1 deficiency, it reduced the extent of white matter inflammation in the brain.

nodes.description: Hepatic dysfunction is a less frequently reported manifestation.

evidence.reference_title: Effects of hematopoietic stem cell transplantation on acyl-CoA oxidase deficiency: a sibling comparison study.
evidence.snippet: facial dysmorphism, hepatic dysfunction, and adrenal insufficiency are less frequently reported findings.

nodes.description: Seizures occur in the early infantile presentation.

evidence.reference_title: Peroxisomal acyl-CoA-oxidase deficiency: two new cases.
evidence.snippet: Early onset hypotonia, seizures and psychomotor delay were observed in both cases.

nodes.description: White-matter demyelination and leukodystrophy are central radiographic and pathological features.

evidence.reference_title: The inflammatory response in acyl-CoA oxidase 1 deficiency (pseudoneonatal adrenoleukodystrophy).
evidence.snippet: Among several peroxisomal neurodegenerative disorders, the pseudoneonatal adrenoleukodystrophy (P-NALD) is characterized by the acyl-coenzyme A oxidase 1 (ACOX1) deficiency, which leads to the accumulation of very-long-chain fatty acids (VLCFA) and inflammatory demyelination.

nodes.description: Acox1-deficient and other peroxisomal beta-oxidation-defect microglial cell models show broad lipid, immune, lysosomal, autophagy, and disease-associated microglial programs, suggesting microglial pathology as an additional contributor to peroxisomal leukodystrophy.

evidence.reference_title: Peroxisomal defects in microglial cells induce a disease-associated microglial signature.
evidence.snippet: In these cell lines, we used RNA-sequencing and identified large-scale reprogramming for genes involved in lipid metabolism, immune response, cell signaling, lysosome and autophagy, as well as a DAM-like signature.

evidence.reference_title: Peroxisomal defects in microglial cells induce a disease-associated microglial signature.
evidence.snippet: In conclusion, the peroxisomal defects in microglial cells not only impact on VLCFA metabolism but also force microglial cells to adopt a pathological phenotype likely representing a key contributor to the pathogenesis of peroxisomal disorders.

experimental_models.description: Mutant microglia model lipid, immune, autophagy, and DAM-like programs.
experimental_models.name: Acox1-deficient BV-2 microglial cell model

nodes.description: ACOX1 deficiency produces progressive central nervous system disease with inflammatory demyelination, cerebral and cerebellar white-matter involvement, cortical and cerebellar atrophy, neuronal loss, and secondary functional decline.

evidence.reference_title: The inflammatory response in acyl-CoA oxidase 1 deficiency (pseudoneonatal adrenoleukodystrophy).
evidence.snippet: Among several peroxisomal neurodegenerative disorders, the pseudoneonatal adrenoleukodystrophy (P-NALD) is characterized by the acyl-coenzyme A oxidase 1 (ACOX1) deficiency, which leads to the accumulation of very-long-chain fatty acids (VLCFA) and inflammatory demyelination.

evidence.reference_title: Effects of hematopoietic stem cell transplantation on acyl-CoA oxidase deficiency: a sibling comparison study.
evidence.snippet: Both patients experienced a fatal neurodegenerative course, with late-stage cerebellar and cerebral gray matter atrophy.

evidence.reference_title: Effects of hematopoietic stem cell transplantation on acyl-CoA oxidase deficiency: a sibling comparison study.
evidence.snippet: Serial brain magnetic resonance imaging in the younger sibling indicated demyelination began in the medulla and progressed rostrally to include the white matter of the cerebellum, pons, midbrain, and eventually subcortical white matter.

nodes.description: Cultured patient fibroblasts model the ACOX1-deficient inflammatory response and demonstrate IL-1 pathway activation and cytokine modulation by MAPK, p38MAPK, and JNK inhibitors.

evidence.reference_title: The inflammatory response in acyl-CoA oxidase 1 deficiency (pseudoneonatal adrenoleukodystrophy).
evidence.snippet: Furthermore, expression of IL-6 and IL-8 cytokines in patient fibroblasts was down-regulated by MAPK, p38MAPK, and Jun N-terminal kinase inhibitors.

nodes.description: Peripheral neuropathy has been described in severe ACOX1 deficiency.

evidence.reference_title: Pitfall in metabolic screening in a patient with fatal peroxisomal beta-oxidation defect.
evidence.snippet: The patient presented with a typical MRI pattern showing pachygyria, perisylvian polymicrogyria, cerebral and cerebellar white matter abnormalities, and facial dysmorphia, progressive psychomotor retardation, deafness, retinopathy, peripheral neuropathy, and infantile seizures strongly indicative for a peroxisomal disorder.

nodes.id: Preserved phytanic acid, pristanic acid, and plasmalogen screening markers

evidence.reference_title: Peroxisomal acyl-CoA-oxidase deficiency: two new cases.
evidence.snippet: Plasma very-long-chain fatty acids were abnormal in both patients, whereas the plasma levels of phytanic acid, pristanic acid, the bile acid intermediates DHCA and THCA, and erythrocyte plasmalogen levels were normal.

readouts.interpretation: Normal phytanic acid, pristanic acid, and plasmalogen screening markers help distinguish isolated ACOX1 beta-oxidation deficiency from broader peroxisomal dysfunction but do not exclude disease.

nodes.description: Affected children have delayed psychomotor development before regression.

evidence.reference_title: Peroxisomal acyl-CoA-oxidase deficiency: two new cases.
evidence.snippet: Early onset hypotonia, seizures and psychomotor delay were observed in both cases.

nodes.description: Retinitis pigmentosa is reported as a common manifestation of ACOX1 deficiency.

evidence.reference_title: Effects of hematopoietic stem cell transplantation on acyl-CoA oxidase deficiency: a sibling comparison study.
evidence.snippet: Retinitis pigmentosa, vision abnormalities, and hearing loss are also common manifestations

nodes.description: Impaired peroxisomal beta-oxidation causes accumulation of very-long-chain fatty acids, including abnormal plasma VLCFA values and deficient C26:0 oxidation in cellular testing.

evidence.reference_title: Clinical, biochemical, and mutational spectrum of peroxisomal acyl-coenzyme A oxidase deficiency.
evidence.snippet: The biochemical hallmark of this disorder is the accumulation of very long-chain fatty acids.

evidence.reference_title: Pitfall in metabolic screening in a patient with fatal peroxisomal beta-oxidation defect.
evidence.snippet: Subsequent biochemical investigation in cultured skin fibroblasts of the patient, however, revealed elevated concentrations of VLCFAs, deficient oxidation of C26:0, but normal oxidation of both phytanic acid and pristanic acid and normal DE NOVO plasmalogen synthesis, indicative for a defect in the peroxisomal beta-oxidation system.

nodes.description: Vision abnormalities and retinopathy can develop during the progressive course.

evidence.reference_title: Effects of hematopoietic stem cell transplantation on acyl-CoA oxidase deficiency: a sibling comparison study.
evidence.snippet: Retinitis pigmentosa, vision abnormalities, and hearing loss are also common manifestations
